summary:
The Mammography Quality Standards Act (MQSA) established federal regulations for screening and diagnostic mammograms, including requirements for equipment calibration, staff qualifications, and facility accreditation. These standardized guidelines have improved overall mammogram quality across all states, including North Carolina by ensuring consistent technical performance and adherence to best practices nationwide.

Note that this is a summary of Agent1’s reasoning only — do not include your own analysis or opinion.
